summary: With an overall rating of 84/100, FundedNext Futures is a futures-focused evaluation platform offering three challenge models — Rapid, Legacy, and Bolt — each with different profit targets and loss limits. FundedNext Futures offers $25K, $50K, and $100K accounts across Rapid and Legacy, and a $50K account on Bolt. All accounts operate in a simulated trading environment using demo accounts. Challenge fees start from $129 for Legacy. The platform covers CME Group futures including currencies, equity indices, metals, energy, and agriculture. FundedNext Futures is a division of the FundedNext brand.
pros: Three challenge models with different risk profiles
No activation fees or monthly charges
Scaling plan increases contract limits as balance grows
No overnight holding requirement keeps risk defined
Wide range of CME Group futures markets
cons: Simulated trading only — not live exchange execution
bestFor: Futures traders who want a choice between multiple challenge models with different risk profiles, and prefer a structured, rules-based simulated trading environment
notIdealFor: Forex or CFD traders — FundedNext Futures is futures-only. Also not ideal for traders who rely on automated trading (EAs prohibited) or want overnight holding

verdictBlocks.heading: Our Final Verdict On FundedNext Futures
verdictBlocks.body:
FundedNext Futures is a simulated futures trading evaluation platform that offers three distinct challenge models — Rapid, Legacy, and Bolt — each with different profit targets and loss limits. This gives traders the ability to choose a program that suits their risk tolerance and trading style.

The platform covers a solid range of CME Group futures including currencies, equity indices, metals, energy, and agriculture. Scaling plans are available for funded accounts, and no activation fees or monthly charges are applied beyond the initial challenge fee.

While the futures division is still developing its own education tools and community features, the core evaluation structure is clear and well-defined. All accounts operate in a simulated trading environment using demo accounts. If you're looking for a futures evaluation platform with multiple challenge options and defined risk rules, FundedNext Futures is worth considering.

faq.q: How are FundedNext and FundedNext Futures different?
faq.a: FundedNext focuses on CFDs, while FundedNext Futures is built specifically for futures traders. FundedNext Futures offers three challenge models (Rapid, Legacy, and Bolt) with CME Group futures covering currencies, equity indices, metals, energy, and agriculture. Both branches operate using simulated accounts with real market pricing. Each has its own rules, challenges, and payout structures.

faq.q: Does FundedNext Futures accept US clients?
faq.a: Yes, FundedNext Futures accepts traders from the United States. Traders in the US can join, complete a futures challenge, and request payouts under the same rules as international participants.

faq.q: What challenge models does FundedNext Futures offer?
faq.a: FundedNext Futures offers three models: Rapid (profit targets of $1,500/$3,000/$5,000 for $25K/$50K/$100K accounts), Legacy (profit targets of $1,250/$2,500/$6,000), and Bolt ($3,000 target on a $50K account). Each model has different trailing loss limits and rules.

faq.q: Is FundedNext Futures simulated or live trading?
faq.a: FundedNext Futures provides simulated trading challenges using demo accounts. The trading environment uses real CME pricing and market conditions, but execution occurs on simulated accounts rather than live exchange accounts.
